ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Ade ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ, ‘Eto bati sɛkɛ̃’ ni, bɔavila lɛtsa a kĩ bati a aakɛna, kitonɛkĩ be flee bɛtɛ bido kĩ Yohanes nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ.”
translated: The Word was with God, and the Word was true, he spoke to the people the words he heard, and John the Baptist, sent by God, came to testify about the light.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Kĩ Yesu sila ɛnɛ́ eto mui a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ binya ade Hũhũ a ka eko nɛ ye akũ ndɛ ayekĩ mblɔduma aka a.
translated: For Jesus taught them the way we believe in him, and he loved the world so much that he gave his only Son so that all who believe in him may not perish but have eternal life.

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: “Ye obi a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Nláanaa,’ lɛmɛ ke liti a, fuwo ye kalɔɛ ade ɛnaa kɔba a.
translated: He called out to Mbuayɛ, "Nláanaa," and told his child, then he went to Kalɔɛ to fetch Kɔbaa there.

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Ade ntɛ odi tani Asɔli Kɔla a ni, ɛtani koe mɔ Baguma a kĩ edzi nɛ koe kamɛ a.
translated: Ade is the child of Asɔli Kɔla, and he went to Baguma's house and took his wealth.

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Kĩ bɛkadã ye a, Yesu akũ dani nɛ be anɛ kamɛ. Ye anɛ kamɛ kata ndɛ suwa aye, ade ye tɛtadiɛ lɛmɛ kɛna fitaa ndɛ ɔhaĩ aye a.
translated: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with Jesus, and the Word was God. The Word was with Jesus in the beginning. All things were made through him, and without him nothing was made that was made.
